body:
Casa Axis by @felipepantone is more than a home – it’s a living study in movement, geometry, and light. Seeing the Smooth Sofa there, it felt less like an addition and more like a continuation, its form speaking the same quiet language of curve, balance, and comfort.

Originally a private residence, Felipe has transformed this dreamy 1970s space-age villa into a shared creative hub, opening its doors to artists and thinkers who blur boundaries as effortlessly as he does. It’s a place that celebrates the process of making, and the freedom that comes with it, housed in a sculptural environment that balances feel and form.

Many thanks to Felipe for the hospitality, and for welcoming us into his inspiring and ever-evolving space ❤️

It’s our birthday, and we want you in the picture 🎉 We’re giving away vouchers and tees with original illustrations by @milenko_illustration:

🎁 3 x winners: €1,500 Tylko voucher + limited-edition long-sleeve t-shirt
🎁 10 x runners-up: limited-edition long-sleeve t-shirt

Here’s how to enter:
✨ Make sure you’re following @tylko
✨ Comment below telling us why you love Tylko and tag a friend
✨ Optional: share a Story or Post with your Tylko furniture — or the space where you’d like one, and tag @tylko
✨ You can also send the photo via DM or email so we don’t miss it 👀

The most creative entries will win. Shelfies, selfies, future shelf dreams – this is your invite to the party 🥂Ends 14.10.

==

Prizes: Three winners will each receive a voucher worth €1,500 to spend at the Tylko online store (tylko.com), valid for 3 months from the date of issue. The voucher cannot be combined with any other discount, promotion or sale on the website. Ten runners-up will each receive a one-size limited-edition long-sleeved cotton T-shirt (subject to availability). body:
We asked Lex Pott — the Dutch designer behind icons like the Twist and Pillar candles — what he thought of the Smooth Sofa. 

@lexpott already has a few Tylko pieces at home, so bringing in the Smooth Sofa felt like a natural next step. We love how it fits right in — both in how it looks and how it works in his everyday setup ✨ 

What stood out to him most were the details that make Smooth more than just a beautiful object: 
◽️ Modular design – easily adapts to shifting spaces and needs 
◽️ Changeable covers – refresh the look anytime, no need to replace the whole sofa 
◽️ Smart comfort – from the rotating pillow to the movable headrest

It’s great to see that for someone who cares deeply about both form and function, Smooth just made sense ❤️

What should we dream up next?

We asked Lex Pott — the Dutch designer behind icons like the Twist and Pillar candles — what he thought of the Smooth Sofa. 

@lexpott already has a few Tylko pieces at home, so bringing in the Smooth Sofa felt like a natural next step. We love how it fits right in — both in how it looks and how it works in his everyday setup ✨ 

What stood out to him most were the details that make Smooth more than just a beautiful object: 
◽️ Modular design – easily adapts to shifting spaces and needs 
◽️ Changeable covers – refresh the look anytime, no need to replace the whole sofa 
◽️ Smart comfort – from the rotating pillow to the movable headrest

It’s great to see that for someone who cares deeply about both form and function, Smooth just made sense ❤️

body:
Meet @giseleazad – VOGUE columnist and nature dweller – who recently traded city life for the calm and creative challenge of the forest. Now, she’s building three spaces from the ground up on a wild stretch of land – shaping a new kind of home, close to nature and infused with intention.

“Living so freely amongst the trees has changed my perspective on how I want my living space to be. I want wide, open spaces and as many big windows as possible. Like a warm nest in the woods.” From the floorplan to the footprint, every detail is a reflection of her vision: a blank canvas, with the forest as the focal point.

Meet @giseleazad – VOGUE columnist and nature dweller – who recently traded city life for the calm and creative challenge of the forest. Now, she’s building three spaces from the ground up on a wild stretch of land – shaping a new kind of home, close to nature and infused with intention.

“Living so freely amongst the trees has changed my perspective on how I want my living space to be. I want wide, open spaces and as many big windows as possible. Like a warm nest in the woods.” From the floorplan to the footprint, every detail is a reflection of her vision: a blank canvas, with the forest as the focal point.

What should we dream up next?

This competition is not sponsored, endorsed or administered by, or associated with, Instagram. Full terms via the link in bio.
